hero.question: Milwaukee Brewers vs. Boston Red Sox
hero.prob_take: At 8.0%, the market is pricing a Brewers win as a statistical afterthought, which means even a modest bullish catalyst swings the expected value hard for anyone willing to hold overnight. The Red Sox are favored but not by enough to justify near-elimination odds on Milwaukee, especially in baseball where variance is high and matchup details (starting pitchers, bullpen usage) move win probability more than these crowd prices reflect.

daily_take.headline: Brewers odds collapse 36 points in 24 hours, nobody knows why yet.
daily_take.deck: Milwaukee dropped from 44.5% to 8.0% overnight on $801K volume, a catastrophic repricing with no obvious catalyst. Either sharp money just loaded the other side with real information, or this is panic selling into thin liquidity. Smart play: wait for the actual reason before chasing, then fade the overreaction. The odds move this hard for a reason.
